title: Why Jawlines Are So Pivotal To Male Beauty
filename: Why Jawlines Are So Pivotal To Male Beauty.en.vtt
excerpt: Probably one of the things you see quite sadly happening to a lot of clients who want a more defined jawline is they also end up getting a more masculine jawline in terms of male beauty that jawline is like cheeks are for women so you do want the jaw to be more dominant in a male…

segments.text: probably one of the things you see quite sadly happening to a lot of clients who want a more defined jawline is they also end up getting a more masculine jawline in terms of male beauty that jawline is like cheeks are for women so you do want the jaw to be more dominant in a male face and cheeks to be more dominant in a female face and jawlines are um it's the angle here is sharper so you'll go in your anger angle tends to be more a less a more acute angle in a male and it's obviously projected outwards more because it needs to match your cheek so it's slightly broader and then some really subtle stuff that goes with masculinity is to do with the outward curve of the jaw so if you're projecting the jawline out when you're injecting you're making someone more masculine which is probably one of the things you see quite sadly happening to a lot of clients who want a more defined jawline is they also end up getting a more masculine jawline so the pictures look amazing from the side because you have that wonderful shadow but as soon as the head looks straight at you see this extra projection because it's very hard to get definition without
adding volume and if you add volume to the width of your jaw you look more masculine

transcript: probably one of the things you see quite sadly happening to a lot of clients who want a more defined jawline is they also end up getting a more masculine jawline in terms of male beauty that jawline is like cheeks are for women so you do want the jaw to be more dominant in a male face and cheeks to be more dominant in a female face and jawlines are um it's the angle here is sharper so you'll go in your anger angle tends to be more a less a more acute angle in a male and it's obviously projected outwards more because it needs to match your cheek so it's slightly broader and then some really subtle stuff that goes with masculinity is to do with the outward curve of the jaw so if you're projecting the jawline out when you're injecting you're making someone more masculine which is probably one of the things you see quite sadly happening to a lot of clients who want a more defined jawline is they also end up getting a more masculine jawline so the pictures look amazing from the side because you have that wonderful shadow but as soon as the head looks straight at you see this extra projection because it's very hard to get definition without adding volume and if you add volume to the width of your jaw you look more masculine
